name: Agent Summarizing
description: Summarize instructions using a file path and output directory.
expression: Use my instruction in {FILE_PATH} and summarize this in {OUTPUT}/{DATE}.txt

variables.name: FILE_PATH
variables.description: Path to the instruction file

variables.name: OUTPUT
variables.description: Output directory

name: Tree Structure
description: Generate a folder tree structure.

variables.name: FOLDER
variables.description: The folder to generate the tree for

name: List Python Files
description: List all python files in the current directory.

name: Run Mypy
description: Run mypy on a specific path.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable in the virtual environment

variables.name: PATH
variables.description: Path to run mypy on

name: Apply Intelag Python Rules to TOML
description: Apply STANDARD INTELAG RULES from .intelag/python/pyproject.toml into the active or selected pyproject.toml. Right-click a .toml file or run with a TOML file open.

name: Format and Lint
description: Install dependencies and run absolufy-imports, isort, and ruff.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable in the virtual environment

variables.name: EXCLUDE_PATTERNS
variables.description: Comma-separated list of patterns to exclude

variables.name: PATH
variables.description: Path to the directory to format and lint

name: MonkeyType (Script)
description: Install monkeytype, run a specific script to collect types, and apply them to all discovered modules.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable in the virtual environment

variables.name: SCRIPT
variables.description: The script to run to collect types

variables.name: PATH
variables.description: Path to the directory containing modules to apply types to

name: MonkeyType (Directory)
description: Install monkeytype and apply existing type stubs to all discovered modules.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable in the virtual environment

variables.name: PATH
variables.description: Path to the directory containing modules to apply types to

name: Find Dead Code (Vulture)
description: Run vulture to find unused code in a directory, saving the report to .intelag/issues/vulture_report.txt.
expression: python .intelag/commands/scripts/_venv_utils.py --deps vulture && {PYTHON_EXE} -c "import os; os.makedirs('.intelag/issues', exist_ok=True)" && {PYTHON_EXE} -m vulture {PATH} --exclude {EXCLUDE_PATTERNS} > .intelag/issues/vulture_report.txt

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable in the virtual environment

variables.name: EXCLUDE_PATTERNS
variables.description: Comma-separated list of patterns to exclude

variables.name: PATH
variables.description: Path to the directory to scan for dead code

name: Docformatter
description: Run docformatter on a directory recursively, forcing multi-line summaries.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable in the virtual environment

variables.name: EXCLUDE_PATTERNS
variables.description: Comma-separated list of patterns to exclude

variables.name: PATH
variables.description: Path to the directory

name: Ruff Check and Fix
description: Run ruff with --fix.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable in the virtual environment

variables.name: EXCLUDE_PATTERNS
variables.description: Comma-separated list of patterns to exclude

variables.name: PATH
variables.description: Path to the directory to lint and fix

name: Autoflake
description: Remove unused imports and variables using autoflake.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable in the virtual environment

variables.name: EXCLUDE_PATTERNS
variables.description: Comma-separated list of patterns to exclude

variables.name: PATH
variables.description: Path to the directory

name: Full Check (MonkeyType, Format, Lint, Dead Code, Docformatter)
description: Runs MonkeyType, Autoflake, Format and Lint, Find Dead Code and Docformatter in sequence.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable in the virtual environment

variables.name: EXCLUDE_PATTERNS
variables.description: Comma-separated list of patterns to exclude

variables.name: PATH
variables.description: Path to the directory

name: Bump Version
description: Bump version in pyproject.toml (patch, minor, or major) with optional dry-run.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable in the virtual environment

variables.name: BUMP_LEVEL
variables.description: Version bump level: patch (1), minor (2), or major (3)

variables.name: TOML_PATH
variables.description: Path to pyproject.toml file

variables.name: DRY_RUN
variables.description: Add --dry-run to preview without changes, or leave empty

name: Open Focused File in New Tab
description: Open the currently active file in a new side-by-side tab.

name: Git: Find Large Files
description: Analyze the repository and list files larger than X MB.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

variables.name: MIN_SIZE_MB
variables.description: Minimum file size in MB to list

variables.name: OUTPUT_FILE
variables.description: Output file path for the report

name: Git: Remove Files from History
description: Remove specified files from git history using git-filter-repo.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

variables.name: FILES_TO_REMOVE
variables.description: Space-separated list of file paths to remove (e.g. 'path/to/file1 path/to/file2')

variables.name: OUTPUT_FILE
variables.description: Output file path for the log

name: Security: Obfuscate Scripts (PyArmor)
description: Protect your Python scripts by obfuscating them with PyArmor.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

variables.name: SOURCE_DIR
variables.description: Directory containing scripts to obfuscate

variables.name: OUTPUT_DIR
variables.description: Output directory for the secured scripts

name: Rename Codebase: Classes and Files
description: Rename files, directories, and classes based on a YAML plan.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

variables.name: PLAN_PATH
variables.description: Path to the YAML renaming plan

name: Git: Edit Past Commit
description: Uncommit a previous commit to edit its changes. Uses rebase -i and reset HEAD^.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

variables.name: COMMIT
variables.description: The commit hash or reference to edit (e.g. HEAD~2 or a hash)

name: Git: Rename Repo (Cloud + Remote URL)
description: Rename the repo on the cloud (GitHub via gh CLI if available) and update the local remote URL. Run from repo root.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

variables.name: NEW_NAME
variables.description: New repository name (e.g. my-project-v2)

variables.name: REMOTE
variables.description: Remote to update

variables.name: SKIP_CLOUD
variables.description: Only update local remote URL (skip renaming on GitHub)

name: Git: Clone A Folder (Push)
description: Turn an existing folder into its own Git repo and push to an already-created GitHub repo.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

variables.name: FOLDER
variables.description: Path to the folder to turn into a repo (relative to workspace or absolute)

variables.name: REPO_URL
variables.description: GitHub repo URL (e.g. https://github.com/org/repo.git)

name: Git: Clone A Folder (Push & Add as Submodule)
description: Turn a folder into its own Git repo, push to GitHub, then add it back to the main project as a git submodule.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

variables.name: FOLDER
variables.description: Path to the folder to turn into a repo (relative to workspace or absolute)

variables.name: REPO_URL
variables.description: GitHub repo URL (e.g. https://github.com/org/repo.git)

name: Security: GitGuardian Secret Scan
description: Scan the repository for secrets using GitGuardian (requires 'ggshield auth login').

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

variables.name: PATH
variables.description: Path to the repository to scan

variables.name: OUTPUT_FILE
variables.description: Output file path for the report

name: Security: TruffleHog Deep Scan
description: Deep scan git history for high-entropy secrets.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

variables.name: PATH
variables.description: Path to scan (default current repo)

variables.name: OUTPUT_FILE
variables.description: Output file path for the report

name: Security: Remove Secret from History
description: Rewrite git history to remove a specific secret string (Destructive!).

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

variables.name: PATH
variables.description: Path to the repository

variables.name: SECRET_TEXT
variables.description: The exact secret string to remove

name: Security: Git Sanity Check (Hardening)
description: Install pre-commit hooks, update .gitignore, and create .env template.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

name: New Agent Request
description: Create a new agent request file from a template.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable

variables.name: TEMPLATE_FILE
variables.description: Select a template file

variables.name: TEMPLATE_NAME
variables.description: Select a template section

variables.name: FILENAME
variables.description: Enter name for the request file

variables.name: CLIPBOARD_PROMPT
variables.description: The prompt template to copy to clipboard (use {FILE_PATH} for the file path)

name: Git Branch Tree
description: Visualize Git branch relationships in a tree/forest plot using matplotlib.

variables.name: PYTHON_EXE
variables.description: Path to the Python executable in the virtual environment

variables.name: REPO
variables.description: Path to the git repository

name: Activate .venv (PowerShell)
description: Activate the local .venv in the current PowerShell terminal.
expression: if (Test-Path ".venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1") { .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1 } else { Write-Host "[Intelag] .venv not found at .venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1" -ForegroundColor Red; exit 1 }
